items.title: 'Anderlecht polst Overmars', Vinícius zat continu in inbox Mbappé
items.summary: We zitten in een periode tussen twee transferwindows, maar de stroom aan transfernieuws houdt nooit op. Traditiegetrouw volgt Voetbal International de buitenlandse markt op de voet. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in Nederland.

items.title: Toney outscoring Ronaldo, hopes for WC return
items.summary: Ivan Toney is dreaming of winning the World Cup with England as he attempts to force his way into Thomas Tuchel's squad.

items.title: FA Cup fifth-round draw – live
items.summary: ⚽ FA Cup draw news, due to begin from after 6.30pm GMT ⚽ Live scores | FA Cup review | And you can email Niall TNT have kicked off with a walk-and-talk around a packed Macclesfield dressing room, the only problem being that the camera lens keeps steaming up. Let’s get on with it, shall we? Three minutes until the draw, according to an on-screen countdown that will inevitably prove to be inaccurate. I think the fifth round is my personal favourite round of the Cup; close enough to Wembley but still with plenty of room for surprises. Continue reading...

items.title: Sparta breekt contract doorgebroken talent Martes open
items.summary: Sparta Rotterdam gaat langer door met Lushendry Martes (17). De rechtsback, die ook als linksback en centrale verdediger kan spelen, zal per 1 juli tot medio 2030 aan de club verbonden zijn.

items.title: Serie A referee La Penna told to stay at home by police after dozens of death threats
items.summary: La Penna wrongly sent off Juve’s Kalulu against Inter Official could face one-month ban following incident The referee Federico La Penna has received dozens of death threats after wrongly sending off a Juventus player at Inter on Saturday. Italian police have reportedly advised him not to leave his home. La Penna sparked fury among Juventus supporters after dismissing Pierre Kalulu, showing the defender a second yellow card for a challenge on Alessandro Bastoni. Replays showed Bastoni had clearly simulated the fall. Juventus officials and fans argued that the decision heavily influenced the game, which Inter won 3-2, despite the Bianconeri having fought back to level the score with 10 men. Continue reading...

items.title: Macclesfield v Brentford: FA Cup fourth round – live
items.summary: ⚽ FA Cup fourth-round news from the 7.30pm GMT kick-off ⚽ Live scores | FA Cup fifth round draw | And mail Xaymaca Before the match tonight, we have the small matter of the FA Cup draw for the fifth round. Who will Macclesfield or Brentford face in the next round? Niall McVeigh is providing updates. Hello and welcome to a historic minute-by-minute. Tonight sixth-tier Macclesfield welcome Premier League Brentford to the Leasing.com Stadium in the fourth round of the FA Cup. Continue reading...

items.title: Referee and assistant stood down after FA Cup mistakes
items.summary: Referee Chris Kavanagh and his assistant Nick Greenhalgh have not been selected for a Premier League game this weekend.

items.title: FA Cup fifth-round draw LIVE! Newcastle to host Man City
Dit is het programma in de tussenronde van de Champions League
items.summary: Deze week wordt de Champions League hervat met de tussenronde. Hoewel er geen Nederlandse clubs meer actief zijn in het toernooi, staan er nog genoeg aantrekkelijke affiches op het programma.

items.title: Kavanagh stood down from Premier League round after errors in Villa loss to Newcastle
items.summary: Referee Chris Kavanagh will not officiate this weekend VAR was not used in fourth round of FA Cup ties The referee Chris Kavanagh will not officiate in the next round of Premier League fixtures after a series of high-profile errors during the FA Cup tie between Aston Villa and Newcastle on Saturday. Kavanagh has been stood down by Professional Game Match Officials, alongside one of his two assistants at Villa Park, Nick Greenhalgh. The second, Gary Beswick, will run the line at Nottingham Forest v Liverpool on Sunday. Continue reading...

items.title: Bliksemvertrek Heitinga bij Tottenham: 'Tudor kan niks met hem'
items.summary: John Heitinga is niet langer de assistent-trainer van Tottenham Hotspur. Half januari werd Heitinga als assistent van Thomas Frank aangesteld bij de nummer zestien van de Premier League, maar ruim een maand later zit deze klus er dus alweer op. In de nieuwe aflevering van Rondje Buitenland praten Süleyman Öztürk en Bas van den Hoven over de toekomst van Heitinga.

items.title: Macclesfield's Rooney: 'Ambition' to manage higher
items.summary: John Rooney is clear in his ambition to manage at the highest level possible but he wants to go on that journey with Macclesfield.

items.title: Fàbregas hits out at his own striker Morata for red
items.summary: Como coach Cesc Fàbregas did not hold back when criticising Álvaro Morata after the veteran forward was sent off.

items.title: Mourinho talks down Madrid return rumours
items.summary: Benfica coach Jose Mourinho has denied speculation of a potential return to Real Madrid ahead of tomorrow's Champions League playoff opener between the sides.

items.title: Heitinga na ruim een maand al vertrokken bij Tottenham
items.summary: John Heitinga is vertrokken bij Tottenham Hotspur. Na het ontslag van Thomas Frank heeft de Nederlander zijn contract ingeleverd, is bevestigd aan VI.

items.title: AJ's tragic loss inspired Fury to return to boxing
items.summary: Tyson Fury was inspired to return to boxing by the tragic accident that saw Anthony Joshua lose two of his closest friends.

items.title: Raducanu suffers three-set defeat to lucky loser Ruzic in Dubai
items.summary: Emma Raducanu was ousted from the Dubai Tennis Championships by lucky loser Antonia Ruzic as sickness and illness continued to take their toll on the British No 1.

items.title: 'Golazo!'- why one of football's most iconic intros has returned
items.summary: BBC Sport looks at a feature of Serie A coverage which is delighting fans once more, three decades after the height of its popularity.

items.title: Barcelona to remain on F1 calendar until 2032 in rotation with Belgian GP
items.summary: The Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya will remain on the Formula 1 calendar until 2032 after a deal was agreed for the event to feature in a yearly rotation with the Belgian Grand Prix.

items.title: Football Daily | Rangers, Hearts and Celtic set up a Scottish title race for the ages
items.summary: Sign up now! Sign up now! Sign up now? Sign up now! Back in September, when the Rangers website revealed the club “were thrilled to announce” the signing of a Portuguese striker from Everton for £8m, the reaction from fans could only have been more downbeat if the player in question had been Beto. On Reddit, one overwrought supporter described the deal to bring Youssef Chermiti to Ibrox as being “everything that is wrong with football”, noting that the 21-year-old had “rocked up” in Glasgow with nothing to show for his short time in the game except a £5,000 Van Cleef & Arpels bracelet and “teeth straight out of a designer dentist”. (At this point it is probably also worth noting that Football Daily has been knocking around since before Youssef was even a mischievous gleam in his father’s eye and has achieved so little that we’d never even heard of the high-end jewellers until we read the disparaging post in question.) Continue reading...
